data.title: JD Vance, U.S. delegation head to Pakistan for another round of talks - reports
data.description:
Vice President JD Vance, along with the U.S. delegation, is reportedly expected to land in Islamabad, Pakistan, within hours.  

President Donald Trump said he was willing to meet senior Iranian leaders if the talks materialize properly, the New York Post reported. 

“We’re supposed to have the talks… So I would assume at this point nobody’s playing games,” Trump said in a brief interview with the Post. 

Earlier, Iran had said it would not participate in a second round of talks with the United States, according to Iranian state media, which said Tehran objected to what it described as excessive and unrealistic demands from Washington.

Despite Tehran’s announcement, Trump had said U.S. negotiators were prepared to continue talks and planned to travel to Pakistan for another round of meetings.

The diplomatic setback comes as investors monitor disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical route for global oil shipments. Any prolonged impasse between Washington and Tehran could intensify concerns over energy supplies and regional stability.

“Well, I don’t want to get into that with you,” he told the Post when asked if the U.S. would escalate matters if a deal is not reached. “You can imagine. It wouldn’t be pretty,” he had added.

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Central banks may face ‘tyrannical position’ amid Iran war, Mizuho analyst warns
data.description:
Central banks across Asia could be forced to raise interest rates despite weakening economies because the war in the Middle East is putting intense pressure on currencies, according to Vishnu Varathan of Mizuho Bank.

In an interview with CNBC on Monday, Varathan said monetary policymakers may find themselves in a “tyrannical position” where they cannot provide economic support and must instead prioritize currency stability—even at the cost of short-term harm to growth.

“They know from the AFC experience that you can’t let currency stability get away. It’s hard to regain it,” Varathan said, referring to the Asian Financial Crisis of the late 1990s. “So they need to probably hike despite the economy hurting.”

Varathan cautioned investors against premature optimism when oil (USO) (BNO) prices temporarily decline, noting that screen prices do not reflect actual delivery costs. He said access to refined products has become increasingly challenging across Asia (AIA) (EEMA), creating persistent energy and food supply challenges.

“We can’t get prematurely euphoric about any deal signed because the lingering adverse effects mean that we don’t get out of this quickly,” he said.

The analyst also warned of deepening inequality, saying the conflict would push economies “further down the K-shape,” making policy responses increasingly difficult to implement effectively.

data.title: Nasdaq Dividend Preview: 13-year growth streak expected to continue with April hike
Blue Owl Capital Inc. filing confirms co-CEOs no longer backing personal loans with company shares
data.description:
Filings made by Blue Owl Capital  Inc. (OWL) after Friday's close confirmed that co-CEOs Doug Ostrover and Marc Lipschultz adjusted the terms of their personal loans so that the company's shares are no longer pledged as collateral. The Wall Street Journal had previously reported the revised loan arrangements, citing people familiar with the matter.

The alternative asset manager's latest proxy statement said none of its directors or executive officers currently had company stock pledged under loan arrangements. In previous filings, the company said the Ostrover had put up about 43M shares and Lipschultz about 33M shares for collateral against personal loans.

With that much at stake, Blue Owl Capital Inc. (OWL) had previously said that its shares "could decline materially" if the two executives had to sell stock to meet a margin call, Bloomberg News reported.

In the past year, Blue Owl Capital Inc. (OWL) shares fell 43% amid fears that private credit investments in software faced challenges from the proliferation of AI.

In early trading on Monday, Blue Owl Capital Inc. (OWL) shares slipped 0.6% after rising 2.1% on Friday.

data.title: PVH is the most overbought consumer discretionary stock as Q1 earnings roll on
data.description:
As the market moves into the second week of Q1 earnings, we look at the most overbought stocks in the consumer discretionary sector.

Below is a list of the top 10 consumer discretionary stocks ranked based on their RSI. The list includes primary ticker stocks within the sector, featuring companies with market capitalizations ranging from approximately $2.6 billion to over $2.6 trillion.

The list is topped by PVH Corp. (PVH), with an RSI of 81. Crocs (CROX) and Amazon (AMZN) follow closely behind, while Stride, Inc. (LRN) and V.F. Corporation (VFC) round out the top five, both showing an RSI of 76. All ten companies on the list, including Harley-Davidson (HOG), eBay (EBAY), and DICK’S Sporting Goods (DKS), currently maintain RSI values of 73 or higher.

The Relative Strength Index is a momentum oscillator that measures the velocity and magnitude of price changes, plotting them on a scale from 0 to 100. RSI compares the magnitude of recent gains to recent losses over a chosen lookback period, typically 14 days.

Here is the list:

  1. PVH Corp. (PVH), RSI: 81

  2. Crocs, Inc. (CROX), RSI: 78

  3. Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN), RSI: 77

  4. Stride, Inc. (LRN), RSI: 76

  5. V.F. Corporation (VFC), RSI: 76

  6. Tri Pointe Homes, Inc. (TPH), RSI: 75

  7. Harley-Davidson, Inc. (HOG), RSI: 74

  8. eBay Inc. (EBAY), RSI: 74

  9. Choice Hotels International, Inc. (CHH), RSI: 74

  10. DICK’S Sporting Goods, Inc. (DKS), RSI: 73

Consumer Discretionary ETFs: (XLY), (VCR), (FXD), (FDIS), (RSPD), and (RXI)

data.title: Avis Budget is downgraded at Barclays after the sizzling short squeeze rally
data.description:
Avis Budget (CAR) opened trading on Monday higher once again.
 
Earlier in the day, Barclays downgraded Avis Budget (CAR) to Sell following the sharp short squeeze action of the last few weeks. The firm attributed the rally to a supply/demand mismatch, with two holders accounting for 71% of outright ownership of the company and over 100% of economic interest given outstanding swaps. With Avis Budget's (CAR) short interest now at nearly 100% of the float, the short squeeze was set in motion. 
 
"All of this leads to uncertainty about how long this will last and whether CAR stock can go higher," warned analyst Dan Levy. He and his team believe the current stock price is not justified even with the improved car rental fundamentals. The expectation is that the CAR share price will eventually revert, especially if Avis Budget (CAR) issues equity to take advantage of the share price rally.
 
Shares of Avis Budget (CAR) were up 1.8% in early action on Monday and are up more than 400% over the last six weeks.

data.title: Stanley Black & Decker says tariff changes unlikely to affect outlook; shares rise
data.description:
Stanley Black & Decker Inc. (SWK) said Monday it does not expect recent changes to the Section 232 tariff framework to have a material effect on its full-year 2026 guidance.

The maker of tools and outdoor products disclosed the update in a brief statement issued before markets opened.

Shares of Stanley Black & Decker rose 1.6% as regular trading began Monday.

Section 232 tariffs, imposed under U.S. trade law on national security grounds, have affected imports in industries including steel and aluminum. Companies with global supply chains have closely monitored recent revisions for potential cost impacts.

Stanley Black & Decker (SWK) didn't provide additional detail on the tariff changes or whether any mitigating actions were factored into its outlook.

data.title: IBM Dividend Preview: 26-year growth streak expected to continue in April ahead of Q1 results
data.description:
IBM (IBM) is anticipated to announce a dividend increase this April, extending its 26-year streak of consecutive dividend growth.

Based on historical patterns, analysts expect a consensus annual dividend of $6.75 per share, implying a quarterly dividend of $1.6875, which would represent a 0.45% increase from the current $1.68 distribution.

The company last declared a dividend of $1.68 per share in January 2026, representing an annual yield of 2.65%, and raised its dividend last April by 0.6% to $1.68 from $1.67.

The information technology company has delivered a 5-year dividend growth rate of approximately 1.53% and maintains a 4-year average payout ratio of 67.41%.

The IT consulting and other services company holds ratings of B for safety and growth, A- for yield, and A+ for dividend consistency.

IBM (IBM) is scheduled to report its first-quarter 2026 results on Wednesday, April 22, 2026, after market close.

data.title: Phillips 66, Kinder Morgan advance Western Gateway pipeline
data.description:
Phillips 66 (PSX) and Kinder Morgan (KMI) said Monday they will move forward with the proposed Western Gateway pipeline following a successful second open season that secured long-term shipper commitments sufficient to move the project forward.

Western Gateway will connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply to Arizona and California markets with connectivity to Las Vegas, Nevada, through Kinder Morgan's (KMI) CALNEV pipeline; the system will combine new pipeline construction with the use and modification of existing assets, including reversing portions of Kinder Morgan's SFPP pipeline and Phillips 66's (PSX) Gold pipeline to enable westbound flows.

"Customer response during the open season underscores the importance of Western Gateway in addressing long‑term refined products logistics needs in the region," Phillips 66 (PSX) Chairman and CEO Mark Lashier said.

"We're pleased to be able to use our existing assets to leverage growth opportunities for the Arizona and California markets," Kinder Morgan (KMI) CEO Kim Dang.

data.title: Adobe gains after announcing launch of AI agents for businesses
data.description:
Adobe (ADBE) shares gained nearly 2% in premarket trading on Monday after the Photoshop maker said it is releasing a set of AI agents aiming to help corporate customers automate functions including digital marketing.

The product, called CX Enterprise, is an AI agent-based platform that can be used to help businesses “codify their own business processes” and boost areas like customer engagement, sales, and brand visibility.

“The rise of platforms like ChatGPT Enterprise, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, and others is reshaping how work gets done. Adobe CX Enterprise is designed to integrate into this evolving ecosystem, bringing customer experience intelligence into the tools where decisions are made,” Adobe said.

The announcement came as Adobe kicked off its annual digital marketing and customer experience conference in Las Vegas. 

This is Adobe’s latest move as the company is trying to stay ahead in the AI race. The San Jose, Calif.-based Adobe has already integrated AI into its ecosystems through its Firefly AI models.

Recently, the company also launched Firefly AI Assistant, an all-in-one creative AI studio that will let users generate results by describing their needs in natural language prompts.

However, the stock recently faced some uncertainty, including getting caught in a broader selloff. Investors also sold shares in March after the company said its CEO Shantanu Narayen is stepping down after 18 years at the helm, furthering fanning worries over its strategy to battle AI competition.

Shares of the company fell over 30% so far this year, compared to the 4% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.
